blocks:
Ayah

إِنَّآ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ فِى لَيْلَةِ ٱلْقَدْرِ (۱)
Indeed We sent it down on the Night of Ordainment. (1)

Key Points

Allah manages the affairs of the universe through the angels and by their mediation. Therefore, in many verses of the Quran, the verbs and pronouns related to Allah are expressed in the plural form. For instance, at the beginning of this Surah, He says: «انّا انزلناه» (We sent it down).

Based on the view of the exegetes (31), the Quran was revealed twice. Once, as a complete whole on the Night of Qadr (Power), which this Surah refers to, and another time, gradually over the twenty-three years of the Prophet’s mission. The Quranic expressions regarding the revelation of this heavenly Book are also of two types. Some use the word «انزلنا» (Anzalnā) from the root inzāl, meaning instantaneous revelation, and others use the word «نزّلنا» (Nazzalnā) from the root tanzīl, meaning gradual revelation.

By combining the Ayah «انّا انزلناه فى لیلة القدر» (Indeed, We sent it down on the Night of Qadr) with the Ayah «شهر رمضان الّذى انزل فیه القرآن» (The month of Ramadan in which the Quran was revealed) (32), it becomes clear that the Night of Qadr is in the month of Ramadan. However, the Quran does not indicate which specific night of Ramadan it is, and in narrations, it is mentioned as being uncertain among three nights. For instance, when Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked about the Night of Qadr, he said: Seek it in the nineteenth, twenty-first, and twenty-third nights of Ramadan (33). However, it is well-known among the Ahl al-Sunnah (Sunnis) that the twenty-seventh night of Ramadan is the Night of Qadr.

31) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

32) Al-Baqarah, 185.

33) Wasā’il al-Shī‘ah, Vol. 10, p. 360.

Messages

1- The Night of Qadr is a night of gratitude and thanks to Allah, because the most important blessing of Allah upon humanity—the Quran—was revealed. «انّا انزلناه فى لیلة القدر»

2- The container and the contained must be commensurate. The best Book was revealed on the best night to the best of mankind. «انّا انزلناه فى لیلة القدر»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Qadr
Surah Al-Qadr was revealed in Mecca, and like other Meccan Surahs, its verses are short and rhythmic. This Surah begins by referring to the revelation of the Quran on the Night of Qadr, continues by explaining the importance of the Night of Qadr to Allah and its superiority over a thousand months, and concludes with divine peace and salutations on that night.
Numerous narrations have been reported regarding the virtue of reciting this Surah after Surah Al-Fatiha in prayer. The one who recites it in an obligatory prayer is addressed: «غفر اللّه لك ما مضى فاستأنف العمل» (29) (Allah has forgiven your past, so start your deeds anew).
Imam Reza عليه السلام said: Whoever recites Surah Al-Qadr seven times when visiting the graves of the believers, Allah will forgive those deceased and the one who recited the Surah (30).

29) Wasā’il al-Shī‘ah, Vol. 6, p. 79. 30) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Ayahs

وَمَآ أَدْرَىٰكَ مَا لَيْلَةُ ٱلْقَدْرِ (۲)
And what will show you what is the Night of Ordainment? (2)
لَيْلَةُ ٱلْقَدْرِ خَيْرٌ مِّنْ أَلْفِ شَهْرٍ (۳)
The Night of Ordainment is better than a thousand months. (3)

Key Points

The word Qadr (قدر) is used in the Quran in several meanings:

A) Status and dignity. As He says: «و ما قدروا اللّه حقّ قدره»(34) They did not estimate Allah with the estimation due to Him.

B) Decree and destiny. As He says: «جئت على قدر یا موسى»(35) O Moses, you have come according to [Our] decree (to this sacred place).

C) Straitness and difficulty. As He says: «و من قدر علیه رزقه...»(36) But he whose provision is restricted...

The first two meanings are suitable for Laylat al-Qadr (The Night of Decree/Power), because the Night of Qadr is both a night of status and a night of decree and destiny.

Existence has calculation, measure, and limit. «و اِن مِن شى ء الاّ عندنا خزائنه و ما ننزّله الاّ بقدر معلوم»(37) There is not a thing but that with Us are its treasuries, and We do not send it down except according to a known measure. Even the amount of rain descending from the sky is calculated. «و انزلنا من السماء ماء بقدر»(38) Not only rain, but everything has a measure with Him. «و كلّ شى ء عنده بمقدار»(39) Just as the sun and the moon have precise calculation regarding their volume, weight, movement, and orbit. «و الشمس و القمر بحسبان»(40)

Allah decrees the affairs of one year during Laylat al-Qadr, just as the Quran says elsewhere: «فیها یفرق كلّ امر حكیم»(41) In that night is made distinct every precise matter. Therefore, Laylat al-Qadr is not restricted to the night the Quran was revealed or the era of the Prophet, but rather, in every Ramadan, there is a Laylat al-Qadr where the affairs of the coming year until the next Laylat al-Qadr are measured and decreed.

Staying awake during Laylat al-Qadr and reviving it with supplication, prayer, and Quran was highly recommended by the Prophet and his Ahl al-Bayt (عليه السلام). Among the three nights, they placed greater emphasis on the twenty-third night. For instance, a person asked the Prophet: "Our home is far from Medina. Specify one night so that I may come to the city." The Prophet said: "Enter Medina on the twenty-third night." (42)

Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام), while ill, requested that they take Him to the mosque on the twenty-third night (43), and Lady Fatimah (عليها السلام) would prevent her children from sleeping on this night by sprinkling water on their faces. (44)

It is narrated in traditions that during the last ten days of the month of Ramadan, the Prophet would fold up His sleeping mat and revive those ten nights. (45)

In a lengthy tradition from the Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, we read that Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) submitted to God: O God, I seek the station of nearness. The reply came: «قُرْبى لِمَن اسْتَیْقَظَ لَیْلَةَ الْقَدْر» My nearness is for him who stays awake on Laylat al-Qadr.

He submitted: My Lord, I seek Your mercy. The reply came: «رَحْمَتى لِمَنْ رَحمَ الْمَساكِینَ لَیلةَ الْقَدر» My mercy is for him who shows compassion to the poor on Laylat al-Qadr.

He said: O God, I want permission to cross the Sirat (bridge). The reply came: «ذلِكَ لِمَنْ تَصَدَّقَ بِصَدَقَةٍ لَیْلَةَ الْقَدْر» The key to crossing the Sirat is giving charity on Laylat al-Qadr.

He submitted: O God, I seek Paradise and its blessings. The reply came: «ذلك لِمَنْ سَبَّحَ تَسْبیحَة فى لیلةِ القدر» Attainment of that depends on saying a Tasbih (glorification) on Laylat al-Qadr.

He submitted: My Lord, I seek salvation from the Fire of Hell. The reply came: «ذلك لِمَنْ اسْتَغْفَرَ فِى لَیلةِ الْقَدْر» The key to salvation from Hell is seeking forgiveness (Istighfar) on Laylat al-Qadr.

Finally, He said: O God, I seek Your pleasure. The reply came: «رِضاىَ لِمَنْ صَلّى رَكْعَتَینِ فِى لَیلةِ الْقَدْر» He who prays two units (rak'atayn) on Laylat al-Qadr is included in My pleasure. (46)

Among the strange historical events is the striking of Imam Ali (عليه السلام) on Laylat al-Qadr, in the prayer niche (mihrab) of worship, during prayer. Indeed, the noblest of God's creation was martyred in the noblest of places, at the noblest of times, and in the noblest of states.

The night holds a special position in spiritual matters, of which we mention a few:

Allah summoned Moses (عليه السلام) for night communion to bestow the Torah: «اربعین لیلة»(47)

The Quran considers the time of dawn (Sahar) suitable for seeking forgiveness: «و بالاسحار هم یستغفرون»(48)

The Prophet's ascension (Mi'raj) to the heavens was during the night. «اسرى بعبده لیلاً من المسجد الحرام»(49)

The Prophet was commanded to engage in night communion and worship. «و من الّیل فتهجّد به نافلة لك»(50), «قم الّیل الاّ قلیلا»(51)

Allah praises those who worship at night. «یتلون ایات اللّه اناء الّیل»(52) And He recommends glorification at night. «فسبّحه لیلا طویلا»(53)

Allah swears in the Quran once by the time of dawn (Fajr) and the time of afternoon ('Asr), but three times by the time of night/darkness: «و الّیل اذا عسعس»(54), «و الّیل اذ ادبر»(55), «و الّیل اذا یسر»(56)

The duration of Laylat al-Qadr is one complete night of the Earth, which is 24 hours, not that it is specific to a particular place like Mecca and limited to the 8 hours of night in that region. Just as the intent of the day of Eid al-Fitr is one complete day of the Earth, encompassing all points and regions.

The coincidence of the night of human destiny with the night of the Quran's revelation is perhaps a secret indicating that human destiny is dependent on the Quran. If he follows the Quran, happiness and salvation are decreed for him; and if he is far from the Quran, wretchedness and misery are decreed for him.

Abu Dharr says: I submitted to the Messenger of Allah, "Was Laylat al-Qadr present during the time of the Prophets, and were affairs revealed to them, and after they passed away, did the revelation of affairs on that night cease?" The Prophet said: "Laylat al-Qadr exists until the Day of Judgment." (57)

Perhaps the secret behind Laylat al-Qadr being hidden is so that people engage in worship on multiple nights, those who realize one night do not become arrogant, and those who miss it do not despair of the remaining nights.

It is narrated in traditions: «العمل الصالح فیها خیر من العمل فى الف شهر لیس فیها لیلة القدر»(58) Good deeds performed therein are better than deeds performed in a thousand months in which there is no Laylat al-Qadr.

34) Al-An'am, 91. 35) Taha, 40. 36) At-Talaq, 7. 37) Al-Hijr, 21. 38) Al-Mu'minun, 18. 39) Ar-Ra'd, 8. 40) Ar-Rahman, 5. 41) Ad-Dukhan, 4. 42) Wasa'il ash-Shi'ah, vol. 3, p. 307. 43) Bihar al-Anwar, vol. 95, p. 169. 44) Bihar al-Anwar, vol. 94, p. 10. 45) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 46) Wasa'il ash-Shi'ah, vol. 8, p. 20. 47) Al-Baqarah, 51. 48) Adh-Dhariyat, 18. 49) Al-Isra, 1. 50) Al-Isra, 79. 51) Al-Muzzammil, 2. 52) Al 'Imran, 113. 53) Al-Insan, 26. 54) At-Takwir, 17. 55) Al-Muddaththir, 33. 56) Al-Fajr, 4. 57) Tafsir Burhan. 58) Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih, vol. 2, p. 158.

Messages

1- Choose a sacred time for sacred matters. «لیلة القدر خیر من الف شهر» Laylat al-Qadr is better than a thousand months.

2- Times are not equal; some times have superiority over others. «لیلة القدر خیر من الف شهر» Laylat al-Qadr is better than a thousand months.

Ayah

تَنَزَّلُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ وَٱلرُّوحُ فِيهَا بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِم مِّن كُلِّ أَمْرٍ (٤)
In it the angels and the Spirit descend, by the leave of their Lord, with every command. (4)

Key Points

They asked Imam Sadiq عليه السلام: Is the Spirit (Ruh) the same as Gabriel (Jibril)? He replied: Gabriel is one of the angels, and the Spirit is superior to the angels. Then He recited this Ayah: «تنزل الملائكة والروح...» (59)

With the descent of the angels upon the heart of the Prophet and the infallible Imam on the Night of Qadr, a connection is established between the earth and the heaven, the physical realm (Mulk) and the spiritual realm (Malakut), and nature and the supernatural.

The Spirit (Ruh) has several types: - A spirit that is the source of desires and lusts. - A spirit that is the source of power and movements. These two spirits are shared between humans and animals. - A spirit that is the source of choice and thought, which is exclusive to humans. - A spirit that is pure intellect and constitutes the essence of the angels' existence. - A spirit that is superior to the angels and descends to earth with them on the Night of Qadr.

It is mentioned in narrations that you should argue against the deniers of the Imamate using Surah Al-Qadr (60). This is because, based on this Surah, the angels descend every year on the Night of Qadr. During the lifetime of the Prophet, they descended upon his noble being. After him, upon whom do they descend? It is clear that not everyone can host the Divine angels, and that person must be infallible, like the Prophet, and entrusted with the guardianship (Wilayah) of human affairs. That person is none other than the infallible Imam, who in our time is Hadrat Mahdi عليه السلام, and the angels attain the honor of visiting His presence on the Night of Qadr.

A Look at the Angels in the Quran (61) Angels are the agents of the cosmic order and are commissioned to carry out the commands of Allah in the administration of affairs. In the Quran, a specific mission and role have been assigned to every group of angels, and they have been named according to that mission: «النّازعات» (62), «الصّافات» (63), «الزّاجرات» (64), «النّاشرات» (65), «الفارقات» (66), «المدبّرات» (67)

Some angels are appointed for protection. «انّ علیكم لحافظین» (68) Some angels converse with the Divine saints. They spoke with Zachariah and gave him the glad tidings of a son named Yahya (John). «نادته الملائكة» (69) And these same angels conversed with Hadrat Maryam (Mary). «اذ قالت الملائكة یا مریم» (70) In some instances, there is mention of angels aiding fighters on the battlefronts. For example, in the Battle of Badr, Allah sent three thousand angels to assist the Muslims. «یمدّكم ربّكم بثلاثة ءالاف من الملائكة» (71) Some angels are responsible for taking people's lives. «یتوفّیكم ملك الموت» (72) And another group is responsible for the affairs of the Throne. «یحمل عرش ربّك» (73)

Among the distinctions of the angels is their perpetual and tireless worship, regarding which the Quran states: «و من عنده لایستكبرون عن عبادته و لا یستحسرون» (74), «یسبحون الیل و النهار لایفترون» (75)

Other duties of the angels include praying and seeking forgiveness for the believers «یستغفرون لمن فى الارض» (76) and sending blessings (Salawat) upon the Prophet of Islam. «انّ اللّه و ملائكته یصلّون على النّبى» (77)

Angels have ranks, and not all are at the same level and status. «ما منّا الا له مقام معلوم» (78) Just as Allah has favored some prophets over others, He has also chosen some from among the angels. «اللّه یصطفى من الملائكة» (79) The power of all angels is not equal, and they have different capacities. «اولى اجنحة مثنى و ثلث و رباع» (80) Some are obedient and some are commanders, but all are infallible and trustworthy. «مطاع ثَمَّ امین» (81)

Belief in all angels is necessary, «كلّ آمن باللّه و ملائكته» (82) and their denial is mentioned alongside the denial of Allah. «و من یكفر باللّه و ملائكته» (83)

Angels are the Divine army, and no one knows their number except Allah. «و ما یعلم جنود ربّك الاّ هو» (84) And if sometimes there is mention of their seemingly small number, such as the Ayah «علیها تسعة عشر» (85), which states that the guardians of Hell are nineteen, this is due to other reasons, not a lack of forces.

Angels have absolute obedience to Allah and carry out whatever they are commanded, never disobeying. «لایعصون اللّه ما امرهم و یفعلون ما یؤمرون» (86) They do not precede His command with speech and only act upon His command. «لایسبقونه بالقول و هم بامره یعملون» (87) Their obedience is conscious and based on Divine piety, and in their obedience, they do not choose a specific task, but rather perform whatever task is assigned to them. «یخافون ربهم من فوقهم و یفعلون ما یؤمرون» (88)

The Prophet of Islam said: The creation of the angels is entirely from light. «انّ اللّه عزّ و جلّ خلق الملائكة من النور» (89) And Allah has not created anything in such great number as the angels. «ما من شى ء مما خلق اللّه اكثر من الملائكة» (90) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Angels do not eat, drink, or engage in sexual intercourse. «انّ الملائكة لا یأكلون و لا یشربون و لاینكحون» (91)

59) Tafsir Burhan. 60) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 61) In Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermons 1 and 91 contain material about the angels. 62) An-Nazi'at, 1. 63) As-Saffat, 1. 64) As-Saffat, 2. 65) Al-Mursalat, 3. 66) Al-Mursalat, 4. 67) An-Nazi'at, 5. 68) Al-Infitar, 10. 69) Al 'Imran, 39. 70) Al 'Imran, 42. 71) Al 'Imran, 124. 72) As-Sajdah, 11. 73) Al-Haqqah, 17. 74) As-Sajdah, 15. 75) Al-Anbiya, 20. 76) Ash-Shura, 5. 77) Al-Ahzab, 56. 78) As-Saffat, 164. 79) Al-Hajj, 75. 80) Fatir, 2. 81) At-Takwir, 21. 82) Al-Baqarah, 285. 83) An-Nisa, 136. 84) Al-Muddaththir, 31. 85) Al-Muddaththir, 30. 86) At-Tahrim, 6. 87) Al-Anbiya, 27. 88) An-Nahl, 50. 89) Ikhtisas, p. 109. 90) Tafsir Qummi, Vol. 2, p. 260. 91) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 56, p. 174.

Messages

1- Angels do not perform any action without the permission of Allah. «تنزّل الملائكة... باذن ربّهم»

2- The decreeing of affairs on the Night of Qadr takes place through the descent and presence of the angels. «تنزّل الملائكة... من كل امر»

3- The decreeing of affairs on the Night of Qadr is a manifestation of Divine Lordship (Rububiyyah). «باذن ربّهم من كل امر»

Ayah

سَلَـٰمٌ هِىَ حَتَّىٰ مَطْلَعِ ٱلْفَجْرِ (٥)
It is peaceful until the rising of the dawn. (5)

Key Points

"Salām" (Peace) is one of the Names of Allah in the Quran. «هو اللّه الّذى لا اله الا هو الملك القدوس السلام...»(92) The meaning of "Salām" in this Ayah is the special divine grace and favor shown by Allah towards His servants during Laylat al-Qadr (the Night of Decree), which brings health, mercy, and blessing, and closes the door to vengeance and punishment, because the cunning and temptation of Satan are ineffective on that night.(93) During Laylat al-Qadr, the angels descend to the earth and offer Salām (greetings of peace) to every man and woman who is engaged in worship.(94) Just as in the Hereafter, they will greet the inhabitants of Paradise with Salām: «سلام علیكم طبتم فادخلوها خالدین»(95)

92) Hashr, 23. 93) Tafsir al-Mizan. 94) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 95) Zumar, 73.

Messages

1- Laylat al-Qadr is the night of soundness for the human mind and soul, and ascension towards Allah, the Salām (Source of Peace). «سلام هى حتّى مطلع الفجر»

2- Laylat al-Qadr is the night of mercy, and through repentance, one can attract the favors of Allah to oneself. «سلام هى حتّى مطلع الفجر»

3- The decree of affairs by Allah is based on human felicity, unless man himself wills otherwise. «سلام هى حتّى مطلع الفجر»
